sutta: 6 in the buddha said amongst these 7 thoughts when may become made an increase in a very fruitful of great advantage plumbing the deathless having the deathless as their goal what 7 the thought of the unattractive the thought of death the thought of the distaste or clawing of food the thought of all world discontent of impermanence of dukkha in impermanence of no self in dukkha verily amongst these 7 thoughts when made become made and increasing are very fruitful of great advantage plumbing the deathless having the deathless as their goal monks the thought of the unattractive when made become made and increasing is very fruitful of great advantage plumbing the deathless having the deathness as its goal thus is this said and on what score is this said monks when a man lives much with the thought of the unattractive heaped around the mind the mind draws back bends back turns back from falling into sex ways is not distended thereby and either equanimity or disgust is set up just as a cock's feather or piece of gristle or cartilage thrown on the fire draws back bends back turns back and is not distended even so when a monk lives much with the thought of the unattractive heaped around the mind the mind draws back bends back turns back from falling into sex ways and is not distended thereby and either equanimity or disgust is set up monks if while he so lives the mind flow after such things or sensual things if relish be set up the monk must realize this not made become by me is the thought of the unattractive not by me is there a progress from the old state to a better not 1 by me is the fruit of making become then surely he is thoughtful but if while he so lives the mind draw back bend back turn back from falling into sex ways and be not distended thereby and either equanimity or disgust be set up he ought to realize this may become by me is the thought of the unattractive i have progressed from all to a better state i have won the fruit of making become then surely he is thoughtful monks the thought of the unattractive 1 made become made an increase in is very fruitful of great advantage plumbing the deathless having the deathless as its goal thus this is said and it is on this core it is said monks the thought of death when may become is very fruitful thus is this said and on what scores they said monks when a monk lives much with the thought of death heaped around the mind the mind draws back bends back turns back from longing for life and is not distended thereby etc monks when a monk lives much with the thought of the distaste of clawing of food heaped around the mind the mind draws back from craving for taste monks when a monk lives much with the thought of all world discontent heaped around the mind the mind draws back from the garishness or attractiveness of the worlds monks when a man lives much with the thought of impermanence heaped around the mind the mind draws back bends back turns back from gains fevers and flattery and is not distended thereby and either equanimity or disgust is set up just as a cock's feather or a piece of gristle thrown on the fire draws back bends back turns back even so the mind when a monk lives much with these thoughts heaped around the mind the mind draws back from gains favors and flattery monks when a man lives much with the thought of dukkha or ill in impermanence heaped around the mind and lethargy indolence anger idleness carelessness and disregard come a lively sense of fear springs up as a slayer with drawn sword monks when among lives much with the thought of there being no self in dukkha heaped around the mind the mind is free of ideas that make for eye and mind as to this conscious body as to all outward signs is beyond the vanities or conceits calmed wholly liberated monks if while he so lives the mind be not free of all such ideas nor wholly liberated the monk must realize this not made become by me is the thought of there being no self in dukkah not by me is there a progress from the old state to a better not 1 by me is the fruit of making become then surely he is thoughtful but if while he so lives the mind be free of ideas that make for i and mine as to this conscious body as to all outward signs be beyond the vanities calmed wholly liberated he ought to realize may become by me is the thought of there being no self in dukkha i have progressed from the old state to a better i have won the fruit of making become then surely he is thoughtful monks the thought of there being no self in dukkha when may become made and increasing is very fruitful of great advantage plumbing the deathless having the deathless as its goal thus is this said and it is on this score it is said verily monks these 7 thoughts when made become made an increase in a very fruitful of great advantage plumbing the deathless having the deathless as their goal as the end of the sutta
commentary: here in this this sutra the buddha is telling us about the 7 types of contemplations that we ought to uh or to think about the first 1 is the thought of the unattractive unattractiveness of the body or low slimness of the body and this helps us to reduce our lust our sensual desire and um if we find that we have not progressed if we find that we are still attracted then the buddha said we must be thoughtful we have to cultivate it more but if after cultivating it now we find that the attractive attraction of the opposite sex is not so strong and then you realize that you have progressed from an old state to a better state and the second 1 is the thought of the thought of death i thought that any time we may death may come then if we continually make this contemplation and then the mind turns back from longing for life the third 1 is the thought or perception of distaste of food a tiredness of food and we practice this constantly and then it helps us to draw back from craving for taste and the fourth 1 is discontent for the world if we realize that the world is um it's not so attractive and then uh we draw back from the attractions of the world and the fifth 1 is the thought of impermanence and if we practice this constantly and then the mind draws back from gains fevers have flattery and then the 6 is the thought of dukkha in impermanence if you cultivate this and then 1 day if the mind becomes lethargic in indolent becomes careless then a lively sense of fear springs up to remind us that dukkha is like a slayer with a drawn sword and because of the fear then we become careful again then the seventh 1 is the thought of there being no self in dukkha and if we practice this perception of no no self and then the ideas of i and mine pass away so these 7 thoughts 7 perceptions are quite important and this is sutter number
